chief_complaint: A 58-year-old White Swiss male presented with pleuritic, position-dependent chest pain, dyspnea, and transient left-arm paresthesia.
history: The patient presented with pleuritic, position-dependent chest pain, dyspnea, and transient left-arm paresthesia. Initial workup showed mildly elevated troponins (38 ng/l) and left-sided pleural effusion on X-ray.
exam: Vital signs: not available. Physical examination findings: pleuritic, position-dependent chest pain, dyspnea, and transient left-arm paresthesia.
labs: ECG: not available. Bloods: mildly elevated troponins (38 ng/l). Imaging: left-sided pleural effusion on X-ray, ovoid epipericardial fat lobule with surrounding edematous infiltration on computed tomography.
